title: Phelypaea arenaria
taxonomy: Phelypaea arenaria is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a type of annual or biennial hemiparasitic plant, meaning that it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing other plants. The classification of Phelypaea arenaria is further detailed under the kingdom Plantae and the order Lamiales. This plant is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. Phelypaea arenaria is also known by its synonym Lathraea arenaria.

intro: Phelypaea arenaria, commonly known as sand catchfly, is a rare and endangered plant species belonging to the mallow family (Malvaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and skin conditions, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Phelypaea arenaria include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
